meta.title: X4 Engines — The Complete UK Guide

hero.tagPill: BMW X4 • 2014–Present • 2 Generations (F26, G02) • N20, N55, B48, B58, B47, B57, S58
hero.h1: X4 Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every BMW X4 engine across two generations — from the N20 four-cylinder and N55 six-cylinder of the F26 to the B48, B58 and S58 of the G02. Real UK replacement costs, known failure patterns, and honest ownership economics for BMW's stylish coupé-SUV. Backed by real UK X4 engine enquiries in 2025 [BMW-VERIFIED].

metrics.ourCall: ⚠️ The X4 rule — style over substance: an N20 recon at £2,800–£4,800 against a £6,000–£10,000 F26 is 28–80% — ⚠️ watch. A B48/B58 recon at £3,000–£6,000 against a £15,000–£25,000 G02 is 12–40% — ✅ safe. Generation decides everything.
★★★★☆ — the B48/B58 are the modern gold standard; the N55 is robust; the N20 carries the chain-tensioner caveat
B58B30 (G02 M40i) and B48B20 — the modular B-family engines are the modern gold standard
N20B20 (F26 xDrive28i) without documented timing-chain replacement
2019+ G02 xDrive30i with B48 or M40i with B58 — post-chain-revision, CLAR platform, premium coupé-SUV packaging
G02 X4 M with S58 — 473–503 hp, the full M experience
🏆 The Style-Led SUV — the X3's sportier, coupé-styled sibling

ownershipVerdict.oneLineVerdict: The X4 is BMW's style-led SUV — the X3's sportier sibling with a coupé roofline. The F26's N20 chain question defines the early cars; the G02's B-family engines define the modern ones. Buy on the generation line, not the badge.

H2: BMW X4 Generations — Find Your Vehicle

rows.metric: Sub-headline: Two generations, one coupé-SUV mission. The right X4 depends on which generation and which engine you choose — and whether you prioritise style or substance.
BMW X4 F26 🔥 Most Enquired — N20 drives volume [BMW-VERIFIED]
rows.value: 2014–2018 • N20, N55 — based on F25 X3 platform

Our Verdict: The original X4 — BMW's first compact coupé-SUV, launched in the UK in July 2014. The N20 four-cylinder petrol carries the chain-tensioner caveat; the N55 six-cylinder is robust. The xDrive28i and M40i continued through the end of the 2018 model year. Buy the N55 or an N20 with documented chain history.

rows.metric: BMW X4 G02 🔥 Most Enquired — B48/B58 drives volume [BMW-VERIFIED]
rows.value: 2018–present • B48, B58, B47, B57, S58 (X4 M) — CLAR platform

Our Verdict: The second-generation X4 — built on the CLAR platform shared with the G01 X3. B-family engines only, no N-series carryover. The B48 in the xDrive30i produces 248 hp; the B58 in the M40i produces 382 hp; the S58 in the X4 M produces 473–503 hp. The G02 received a mild facelift in 2021. This is the X4 generation to buy.
The Generation Divide — The Line That Matters Most
Buy N55 or N20 with chain proof

rows.metric: The Badge Ambiguity — "28i" and "30i" Mean Different Things
rows.value: Correction applied (internal): The "28i" badge on the F26 uses the N20B20 — the chain-risk engine. The "30i" badge on the G02 uses the B48B20 — the revised architecture. Both share a similar power output. None share a reliability profile.

engineDatabase.h2: BMW X4 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine ever fitted to a BMW X4 — verified codes, specifications and real UK ownership data.

confidenceScore.title: How confident are these ratings?
confidenceScore.text: N20B20, N55B30 and B58B30 ratings: 90% confidence — these engines are well-documented across the BMW range with clear patterns from specialist rebuilders. N20 timing chain issues are extensively documented. G02 engines: Provisional — the B48/B58 are proven across the wider BMW range but X4-specific data is limited. S58 rating: Pattern-data tier — documented by M car specialist consensus. Data note (internal) — corrections applied: Correction applied: The F26 X4 (2014–2018) uses the N20B20 four-cylinder and N55B30 six-cylinder petrol engines. The N20 timing chain issue is the defining risk. Correction applied: The F26 X4 was not offered in the UK with petrol engines at launch — UK buyers initially had a choice of xDrive20d (188bhp), xDrive30d (255bhp) and xDrive35d (310bhp) diesels. Petrol models were available in other markets. Correction applied: The G02 X4 (2018–present) uses B-family engines only — B48 (xDrive30i, 248 hp), B58 (M40i, 382 hp), B47 (xDrive20d, 190 hp), B57 (xDrive30d, 265 hp) and S58 (X4 M, 473–503 hp). Correction applied: The G02 received a mild facelift in 2021. The X4 M was introduced with the G02 generation.

commonProblems.subHeadline: The six most common failure patterns across the X4 range — symptoms, costs and urgency.

problems.description: The defining F26 X4 issue — the N20's timing chain guides break, causing chain slack and eventual engine failure. Cold-start rattle is the warning. Affects F26 xDrive28i (2014–2018). £2,800–£4,800 recon.
The N20's valve cover gasket and oil filter housing are prone to leaks, leading to oil loss and potential engine damage. Affects F26 xDrive28i. £500–£1,200 repair.
The N55 six-cylinder engine can suffer from high oil consumption and fluid leaks. Affects F26 xDrive35i/M40i. £500–£1,500 repair.
The B48's known weak points — coolant leaks from the water pump, oil filter housing, and expansion tank. Affects G02 xDrive30i. £400–£800 repair if caught early.
The B58's known weak point — coolant expansion tank leaks and water pump failures. Affects G02 M40i. £400–£800 repair if caught early.
Not an engine failure — but a common non-engine X4 enquiry. The F26's xDrive transfer case friction plates wear prematurely. Check on any high-mileage F26 xDrive.

marketIntelligence.h2: BMW X4 Market Intelligence — What UK Owners Told Us in 2025

signals.demandTrend: ⬆ Rising — the F26 is entering affordable territory
⬆ Rising — the G02 is the most popular generation
⬆ Rising — performance buyers seeking the S58

insights: Interesting insight: The X4 generates a different kind of enquiry than the X3 — buyers are often style-led, choosing the X4 for its coupé roofline over the X3's practicality. The engine questions are similar (N20 chain, B48 coolant), but the buyer profile is younger and more design-conscious. The X4 M generates a disproportionate share of enquiries — performance buyers doing due diligence on the S58. (Editorial synthesis — specialist sense-check before publishing.)

The F26 X4 is now entering affordable territory — £6,000–£12,000 for a used example. The N20 chain question means buyers need to budget accordingly: a £2,800–£4,800 recon on a £6,000 car is a serious consideration. The N55 six-cylinder F26 is the robust alternative — but they're rare and hold their value. (Editorial synthesis.)

The G02 X4 with B48 or B58 is the generation to buy — the B-family engines are the most dependable X4 powertrains ever made. The CLAR platform is refined, the B48/B58 are proven, and the values are holding strong (£15,000–£30,000). The X4 M with the S58 is the full M experience — 473–503 hp and track-ready. (Editorial synthesis.)

editorialPullQuote.title: The One Thing We'd Tell Every BMW X4 Buyer
editorialPullQuote.quote: If you buy one X4 based on this page, buy a 2019+ G02 with the B48 or B58 — the B-family engines fixed the chain issue that defined the F26, the CLAR platform is refined, and the coupé styling is distinctive and desirable. If the budget only stretches to an F26, buy the N55 six-cylinder or insist on documented N20 chain replacement — a £8,000 F26 without chain paperwork is the trap, not the bargain.

replacementCosts.h2: BMW X4 Engine Replacement Costs — By Generation
replacementCosts.subHeadline: Real UK pricing for used, reconditioned and rebuilt engines — supply only, with labour guidance.
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Labour estimate: Fitting a BMW X4 engine typically takes 10–20 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [BMW-QUOTE]. The X4 rule — style over substance: An N20 recon at £2,800–£4,800 against a £6,000–£8,000 F26 sits at 35–80% — ⚠️ watch. Against a £15,000–£25,000 G02, a B48/B58 recon at £3,000–£6,000 is 12–40% — ✅ safe. Against a £60,000 X4 M, an S58 recon at £7,000–£10,000+ is 12–17% — ✅ safe. Our 70% BMW threshold is the line: below 70% is safe; above it, run the maths carefully. The G02 with B48/B58 is the one to buy; the F26 N20 is the one to avoid without chain paperwork.

Against a £15,000–£25,000 G02, a B48/B58 recon at £3,000–£6,000 is 12–40% — ✅ safe. Against a £60,000 X4 M, an S58 recon at £7,000–£10,000+ is 12–17% — ✅ safe. Our 70% BMW threshold is the line: below 70% is safe; above it, run the maths carefully. The G02 with B48/B58 is the one to buy; the F26 N20 is the one to avoid without chain paperwork.

engineEvolution.h2: How the BMW X4 Engine Range Evolved — 2014 to Today

eras.whyBmwChanged: BMW launched the X4 as the coupé-styled sibling to the X3. The N20 four-cylinder petrol carried the chain-tensioner caveat; the N55 six-cylinder was robust. In the UK, the F26 was initially diesel-only at launch.
eras.worthKnowing: The F26 is the original X4 — the N55 is the robust pick; the N20 carries the chain risk. Buy the six-cylinder or an N20 with documented chain history.

eras.whyBmwChanged: The second-generation X4 arrived on the CLAR platform — B-family engines only, no N-series carryover. The X4 M with the S58 arrived in 2019. A mild facelift was introduced in 2021.
eras.worthKnowing: The G02 is the X4 to buy — B-family engines are the most dependable X4 powertrains ever made. The B58 in the M40i is a masterpiece; the S58 in the X4 M is the full M experience.

whoShouldBuy.h2: Who Should Buy a BMW X4?

profiles.verdict: The X4 is built for this — the coupé roofline and distinctive design make it the style-led alternative to the X3. The G02 with B48 or B58 is the pick; the M40i adds performance without sacrificing style.
The X4 is a great commuter — the G02 xDrive20d with B47 or xDrive30i with B48 returns good fuel economy, the CLAR platform is comfortable, and the coupé styling makes it stand out.
A cheap F26 N20 is not a cheap X4 — it's an expensive one with the first year's bills prepaid by nobody. A £12,000 G02 with B48 is the bargain; a £6,000 F26 N20 without chain paperwork is the trap.

The G02 X4 M40i with the B58 is a genuine performance SUV — 382 hp, xDrive, and the B58 is one of BMW's most reliable modern engines. The X4 M with the S58 is the full M experience — 473–503 hp and track-ready.
The G02 M40i with the B58 is the modern keeper — the B58 will outlast the car around it. The X4 M with the S58 is the ultimate X4 — a future classic. Both are worth proper investment.

items.question: How much does a BMW X4 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A reconditioned BMW X4 engine typically costs £2,800–£5,500 supply only [BMW-QUOTE] — from the B47/B48 at £2,800–£5,000 to the N20 at £2,800–£4,800. The N55 costs £3,800–£6,000; the S58 (X4 M) costs £7,000–£10,000+. Fitting adds £650–£1,500. Use our calculator for a personalised figure.

items.question: Which BMW X4 engine is the most reliable?
items.answer: The B58B30 straight-six in the G02 M40i and the B48B20 in the G02 — the B-family engines are the modern gold standard. The N55B30 in the F26 is the robust classic pick.

items.question: Which BMW X4 engine should I avoid?
items.answer: No engine outright — but an F26 N20B20 without a documented timing-chain replacement is the one purchase we consistently advise against. A G02 B48/B58 has no such caveat.

items.question: How do I know if my X4 engine needs replacing?
items.answer: By engine: cold-start rattle (N20 timing chain), chain noise under load (N20), coolant loss (B48/B58 cooling system), oil drips (N20/N55 valve cover). On the F26, any rattling on startup is a warning sign. Caught early, it's a repair; ignored, it's a £2,800–£5,500 engine [BMW-QUOTE]. Use our diagnostic calculator for a personalised assessment.

items.question: Is it worth replacing a BMW X4 engine?
items.answer: Depends entirely on generation. Against a £15,000–£25,000 G02, a £3,000–£5,000 B48/B58 recon is 12–33% — ✅ safe. Against a £6,000–£8,000 F26, a £2,800–£4,800 N20 recon is 35–80% — ⚠️ watch. Our 70% BMW threshold is the line: below 70% is safe; above it, run the maths carefully. Use our calculator for your car.

items.question: Which BMW X4 should I buy?
items.answer: 2019+ G02 with the B48 or B58 — the B-family engines fixed the chain issue, the CLAR platform is refined, and the coupé styling is distinctive and desirable. For performance, the G02 M40i with the B58 is the sweet spot. For the budget option, an F26 with N55 — the six-cylinder is robust but rare. Avoid F26 N20s without documented chain history.

items.question: How do I find my BMW X4 engine code?
items.answer: Listed on the VIN plate and in the vehicle documentation; block stamping is visible from below on most engines. Or tell us your generation and variant (F26 xDrive28i, F26 M40i, G02 xDrive30i, G02 M40i) and we'll identify it — 20+ vetted specialists will confirm it for free.

items.question: BMW X4 vs X3 — which is more reliable?
items.answer: Mechanically they share engines across similar eras — the N20/N55/B48/B58 story is identical. The X4 is essentially the X3 with a coupé roofline; reliability is the same. Buy on engine and year, not badge. The X3 offers more practicality; the X4 offers more style. Read the full head-to-head →
